author: Dream
text:
## 2026-04 research update — the "no Apple/Google signing creds" framing needs revision

Deep research on Tauri v2 release best practices (2026-04-10) found the original scope — "only needs GITHUB_TOKEN, no Apple/Google creds" — understates what a production-quality desktop release actually needs. Quick findings and recommendations below; expand scope of this issue or spawn sub-issues as appropriate.

### Distribution channel
GitHub Releases is the **official canonical path** for open-source Tauri apps in 2026. Tauri's own docs have a dedicated `distribute/pipelines/github` page, and the updater consumes a `latest.json` hosted on GitHub Releases out of the box. Real examples anchoring here: Spacedrive, Pot Desktop, Seelen UI, Jan.
Ref: https://v2.tauri.app/distribute/pipelines/github/

### Canonical workflow
- Action: `tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0` (latest v0.6.2, March 2025)
- Matrix: `macos-latest` (ARM default) + `macos-latest --target x86_64-apple-darwin` (Intel cross-compile) + `ubuntu-22.04` + `windows-latest`, `fail-fast: false`
- Trigger: `push: tags: ['desktop-v*']` (prefix because monorepo)
- `permissions: contents: write` + inputs: `tagName: desktop-v__VERSION__`, `releaseDraft: true`, `includeUpdaterJson: true`

### macOS signing — $99/yr is NOT avoidable
Tauri docs state explicitly: *"when using a free Apple Developer account, you will not be able to notarize your application"* — and without notarization macOS Gatekeeper blocks the app on first launch post-10.15. The $99/yr Apple Developer Program membership is the cost floor.

Required secrets (tauri-action pattern): `APPLE_CERTIFICATE` (base64 p12), `APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD`, `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY`, `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD`, and **App Store Connect API key trio** (`APPLE_API_ISSUER`, `APPLE_API_KEY`, `APPLE_API_KEY_PATH`) — preferred over the legacy `APPLE_ID`/`APPLE_PASSWORD`/`APPLE_TEAM_ID` path because API keys aren't tied to a specific Apple ID and rotate cleanly.

### Windows signing — Azure Trusted Signing at $9.99/mo is the 2026 winner
Traditional paths cost $200–700/yr and/or require a hardware token. Azure Trusted Signing (formerly Azure Artifact Signing) launched at **$9.99/mo for 5000 signatures**, chains to the Microsoft ID Verified CS CA, gives instant SmartScreen reputation, and is individual-developer eligible in US/Canada.

Integration: `signCommand` in `tauri.conf.json` + `trusted-signing-cli`. Gotcha: certs are valid only 72 hours so **must** timestamp with `/tr http://timestamp.acs.microsoft.com` or signatures expire.
Refs: https://textslashplain.com/2025/03/12/authenticode-in-2025-azure-trusted-signing/ | https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/artifact-signing/faq

### Linux signing
Basically nothing expected. `.deb` optional GPG, `.AppImage` optional minisign via `SIGN=1` / `SIGN_KEY` env. What matters is the Tauri updater's minisign signature (separate from package signing).

### Tauri updater — mandatory keypair
Tauri v2 ships `@tauri-apps/plugin-updater`. Keypair via `tauri signer generate`, produces minisign-style pair. Public key → `tauri.conf.json` → `plugins.updater.pubkey`. Private key → CI secret `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY` (+ `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD` if set).

**Losing the private key means you can never push updates to existing installs.** Back it up to a password manager AND a hardware vault before first release. This is irreversible.

Endpoint: `https://github.com/<org>/<repo>/releases/latest/download/latest.json` — works when the release is NOT a draft.

### Versioning sync (the unsolved-by-framework problem)
Three files: `Cargo.toml`, `package.json`, `tauri.conf.json`. Recommended pattern:
- Cargo.toml = source of truth
- Omit `version` from `tauri.conf.json` (it defaults from Cargo.toml)
- One tiny `scripts/bump-desktop.mjs` rewrites all three and tags `desktop-v$VERSION`
- Keep desktop version independent from libvex version

### Supply chain (match what protocol-specs did repo-wide)
- `step-security/harden-runner@v2.17.0` with `egress-policy: audit` as first step of every matrix job
- `anchore/sbom-action` to upload an SPDX SBOM per release (covers both JS and Rust halves — consider `cargo auditable` to embed the Rust CycloneDX SBOM in the binary itself)
- `cosign sign-blob --yes` on installers + `latest.json` via keyless/OIDC (`id-token: write`) as a *second* provenance signal on top of Tauri's minisign
- SLSA Level 3 attestations via `slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator`

### Real-world references worth copying
- **Spacedrive** — `spacedriveapp/spacedrive/.github/workflows/release.yml` — self-hosted macOS runners for cert isolation, `APPLE_API_KEY_BASE64` pattern, separate build-then-release jobs
- **Pot Desktop** — minimal clean matrix, good "cheap release pipeline" template
- **Seelen UI** — dual-channel (stable + nightly), Azure Trusted Signing integration

### Estimated recurring cost
**~$219/yr** ($99 Apple + $120/yr Azure Trusted Signing) — versus ~$600–900/yr for Apple + traditional EV cert.

### Scope recommendation for this issue
Either update this issue's scope to reflect the research, or split into sub-issues:
1. `vex-at4.19a` — tauri.conf.json updater keypair + `tauri.conf.json` version handling
2. `vex-at4.19b` — GitHub Actions workflow skeleton (matrix + tauri-action + updater JSON)
3. `vex-at4.19c` — macOS signing + notarization wiring (Apple Developer enrollment, API key secret provisioning)
4. `vex-at4.19d` — Windows signing (Azure Trusted Signing enrollment + signCommand integration)
5. `vex-at4.19e` — SBOM + cosign + SLSA provenance

Linked to parent epic: vex-chat-6gm (vex-chat sibling-repo baseline sync)
